Farn PolIrIcar- PRACTrcps CoMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February 25,2002
CrargMcGlynn
Digiphi Media Group
4809 Windsong Street
Sacramento, CA 95834
Re
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. l-02-023
Dear Mr. McGlynn
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because your question is general in nature and does
not seek advice on a particular slate mailer, we provide you with informal assistance.2
QUESTION
Aside from normal slate mailer committee filing and disclosure requirements,
does the Act prescribe more specific rules governing slate mail presented through the
medium of a CD-ROM?
CONCLUSION
The Act and Commission regulations do not currently prescribe special rules for
slate mailers disseminated through electronic media. If the materials presented in your
anticipated CD-ROM otherwise meet the criteria of "slate fiail," you must comply with
the Act's "conventional" filing and disclosure requirements.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the 
provided by a
Commission opinion or formal written advice. (See enclosed, Regulation 18329(c).)
,ry


File No I-02-023
Page No. 2
FACTS
You expect to produce a slate mailer in a CD-ROM format. The CD-ROM will
be distributed within a vinyl sleeve by United States mail and/or by hand delivery. The
CD-ROM will include basic information pertinent to support or opposition of candidates
andlor issues, additional audio andlor video messages, and links to websites containing
further information on candidates or issues described in the proposed communication.
ANALYSIS
A "slate mailer" is "a mass mailing which supports or opposes atotal of four or
more candidates or ballot measures." (S 82048.3) A "mass mailing" refers to more than
"two hundred substantially similar pieces of mail, but does not include a form letter or
other mail sent in response to an unsolicited request, letter, or other inquiry." ($ 82041 5.)
A sufficient number of CD-ROMs placed in the mail could constitute a "mass mailing,"
and if they contained messages supporting or opposing four or more candidates or ballot
measures, they would be "slate mail" under the Act, even though CD-ROMs differ in
appearance from slate mail in the more traditional (print) medium.
In your request for advice you recognize that these CD-ROMs might be regulated
as "slate mail." We assume that more than 200 substantially similar CD-ROMs would be
placed in the mail, and that they would include a message of support or opposition to a
total of four or more candidates or ballot measures.' If the mailing otherwise meets the
statutory criteria for "slate mail," the "slate mail organization" ($ 82048.4) will incur
reporting obligations prescribed at gg 84218-84221.
In addition to the Slate Mail Organization's reporting obligations, $S 84305.5
and 84305.6 require that certain information be included with each piece of slate mail.
The statutes do not distinguish between print and electronic media, and there are no other
provisions of the Act or Commission regulations requiringthat additional, less or
different information be included in slate mail delivered through "electronic" media.
Until the Commission considers regulatory action, you should ensure that the mailing
envelope andlor outer surface, sleeve or other container can accommodate the disclosures
required under $$ 84305.5 and 84305.6.
3 Please note flrat advocacy for or against "issues" will not cause a mailing to be classified as "slate mail,"
r,vlrich is defined as advocacy of a "slate" of four or more candidates or ballot measures. "Issues" which
are not on flre ballot (in the form of a candidate or ballot measure) will not convert "mail" into "slate mai|."
Of course, any materials not distributed through the mail will not be classified as "slate mail." Unless more
than 200 of your CD-ROMs are mailed out, they will not constitute "slate mail" regardless of content.


File No. I-02-023
Page No. 3
If you have further questions on this matter, please contact me at (9T6)
322-5660

Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By
Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

does the Act prescribe more specific rules governing slate mail presented through the

CONCLUSION: The Act and Commission regulations do not currently prescribe special rules for

anticipated CD-ROM otherwise meet the criteria of "slate fiail," you must comply with
the Act's "conventional" filing and disclosure requirements.
